metrics.narrative: Sasol's RFI-derived business objectives (Business_Objectives__c) name concrete targets: cutting sourcing-plan development from 6 months to 1 month, covering all spend categories with sourcing plans, eliminating PowerPoint/Excel/Word-based plans, and reducing dependency on departing specialists' tribal knowledge. These are quantifiable goals, but no Sasol speaker has voiced them on a call or in an email, and no cost-of-inaction or urgency driver has been named.

champion.narrative: No champion is named in Salesforce and none has emerged in 12 months. Estee Larkins (Snr Commercial Contract Specialist) is the only Sasol contact who replies, but her responses are procedural and guarded: she declined to discuss pricing on 7 Aug 2025 and, on 18 Aug 2025, limited the solutions demo to a strict one-hour, question-driven format ('The intension of this session is for a very brief overview of your solution... We will focus more on the questions we have for you'). This is gatekeeper behavior, not internal advocacy.

competition.narrative: Sasol's current-state stack (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_today field) is SAP Ariba, Taulia, SAP ERP, and an internal AES system for spend reporting. Sasol even routed the RFI itself through the SAP Ariba portal, per the 11 Aug 2025 submission-confirmation email. This makes SAP Ariba the deepest-embedded incumbent to displace, though there is no evidence of a named, actively competing bid inside this specific RFP -- the RFP itself was never issued.

identify_pain.narrative: The same RFI-derived objectives that anchor Metrics also describe the pain: slow (6-month) manual sourcing-plan development, inconsistent document-based strategies, and knowledge loss when specialists leave. No Sasol speaker has personally articulated this pain in a call, and no compelling event (contract expiry, budget cycle, mandate) has been named to create urgency.

sources: Full task/next-step history read, no paper artifacts found
paper_process.narrative: No paper-process artifact (MSA, SOW, Order Form) or discussion has been logged at any point in this deal's life, consistent with a deal that has never advanced past the RFI stage of a Qualified opportunity.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has been identified in 12 months. Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are both unchecked in Salesforce. Hendrik Steynberg (VP Inbound Supply Chain Management), the highest-titled contact on record, attended the 20 Aug 2025 RFI demo and was invited to the Showpad shared space on 22 Aug 2025, but has never individually corresponded with Zycus on budget or commercial terms. Outreach on 14-15 Jan 2026 also targeted Lebelo Lukhele (introduced in that outreach as Sasol's CPO) and Merika Matlou (introduced as Senior Buyer Tactical Procurement) -- neither has replied.

decision_process.narrative: Sasol has kept its internal process opaque. On 7 Aug 2025 Estee Larkins wrote: 'we are more interested in gathering information about your technology offerings based on the SOW... we will need to develop our strategy moving forward... if necessary, we will reach out to you for further discussion once our strategy is finalised.' Eleven months later, no strategy update, RFP, or process step has come back (Next_Step_History__c, 30 Mar 2026 entry: 'unfortunately haven't received any feedback yet').

sources: Task 00TP700000Wwb0IMAR (7 Aug 2025 email from Estee Larkins)
decision_criteria.narrative: Sasol's RFI (issued 14 Jul 2025, deadline 28 Jul 2025) scoped the evaluation to Source-to-Pay (S2P). After the 20 Aug 2025 demo, Zycus logged zero gaps identified during the session ('None at the RFI stage'), suggesting an initially clean fit read. However, no formal Business Requirements Document or scored criteria has been shared back, and Estee Larkins told Zycus on 7 Aug 2025 that Sasol was 'not focusing on pricing' and would 'develop our strategy moving forward' internally before re-engaging -- the actual weighting Sasol will apply remains unknown.

items.name: Stage: Initial Interest → Qualified
items.summary: Opportunity was advanced to Qualified, but this looks procedural rather than earned — it lands on the same day multiple opportunity-health gaps (no decision maker, cold prospect, thin contact map) were flagged, so the stage may be ahead of the actual evidence.

items.name: Evaluation process clarity needed
items.summary: Flagged that we still don't have visibility into how Sasol will evaluate vendors or who influences that process — a basic gap for a deal now marked Qualified.

items.name: Org chart / key contacts missing
items.summary: Health check surfaced that no org chart exists and key contacts across the account haven't been mapped, meaning outreach is likely narrow and exposed to a single thread going dark.

items.name: APD review and approval pending
items.summary: An APD (account plan / discovery document) is awaiting review and sign-off — indicates internal groundwork is still in progress rather than complete.

items.name: Low prospect inbound communication
items.summary: Sasol's inbound engagement has been minimal, reinforcing that we're driving this relationship one-sided right now with little buyer-initiated activity.

items.name: Decision maker not identified
items.summary: No confirmed economic or technical decision maker on the account — a critical blocker given the deal just moved to Qualified.

items.name: Multi-threading gap flagged
items.summary: Engagement is currently single-threaded; the health check calls out the need to widen contacts to reduce risk of the deal stalling if the one active contact disengages.

items.name: Prospect gone cold — 14+ days no inbound
items.summary: No inbound communication from Sasol in over two weeks, the clearest signal that despite the stage bump, we are currently waiting on them with no active dialogue.

day_summary.overall: Sasol moved from Initial Interest to Qualified, but the flurry of health-check flags logged the same day tells the real story: this is a paper advance, not a momentum signal. The account has gone quiet (no inbound in 14+ days), there's no confirmed decision maker, and the org chart/multi-threading work hasn't been done — so the qualification is provisional until basic account intelligence catches up.

cro_panel.intro: Sasol_2025 is a $200K Qualified-stage new-business opportunity that has not advanced since an RFI was submitted in July-August 2025.; The deal has been dark for 175 days against the Salesforce activity rollup and for over 10 months against genuine two-way buyer engagement (last confirmed buyer reply 18 Aug 2025).; No economic buyer, champion, or decision process has been identified, and the RFP Sasol said it would issue in September 2025 was never released

best_practice_check.summary: Salesforce's own automated health check (2026-07-01) already flagged this deal as cold, single-threaded, and missing a decision maker -- confirming the independent read from the activity timeline.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus has been recorded; the last confirmed buyer reply (18 Aug 2025) declined to discuss pricing and kept the demo strictly question-driven.

competitive_position.summary: Competing against SAP Ariba (primary) and Taulia (secondary) in a stalled RFI/RFP cycle that has gone dark for 173 days. Ariba is the entrenched threat given Sasol's likely SAP ERP footprint; Taulia targets working-capital optimization. Zycus has not positioned a differentiated wedge or secured a champion to navigate the competitive dynamics. The path forward is to restart engagement with a head-to-head AI/ML capability comparison (Zycus iAnalyze + iRisk vs Ariba's static analytics) anchored to a quantified pain, delivered via an executive briefing or design workshop.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Sasol_2025 is a $200K Qualified-stage new-business opportunity that has not advanced since an RFI was submitted in July-August 2025. The deal has been dark for 175 days against the Salesforce activity rollup and for over 10 months against genuine two-way buyer engagement (last confirmed buyer reply 18 Aug 2025). No economic buyer, champion, or decision process has been identified, and the RFP Sasol said it would issue in September 2025 was never released.

deal_risk.text: Deal is stuck in Qualified stage for 12 months with the RFI stalled and the promised RFP never issued -- classic early-stage stall risk (weak/no champion, unmapped EB, unclear decision process).
Single-threaded through a guarded, procedural contact who has explicitly declined pricing conversations; no coach or champion exists to restart the stalled process.

win_position.text: No economic buyer engaged in 12 months -- Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are both unchecked, and VP Hendrik Steynberg, the highest-titled contact, has never individually corresponded with Zycus.
No champion, single-threaded relationship -- the only responsive contact, Estee Larkins, is guarded and procedural, declining to discuss pricing on 7 Aug 2025.

Initial technical fit read was clean -- zero gaps were logged from the 20 Aug 2025 solutions demo, and Sasol's RFI-derived objectives (6-month-to-1-month sourcing cycle reduction) map directly to Zycus' GenAI sourcing capability.
RFP promised for Sept 2025 never issued -- Sasol said on 16 Aug 2025 it planned to release the RFP 'early to mid Sept'; as of 30 Mar 2026 the deal's Next Step History still records no traction.

deal_momentum.text: 175 days since the last logged Salesforce activity (15 Jan 2026) and no buyer reply since 18 Aug 2025 (325 days) despite 6+ rep touches (LinkedIn InMails, emails, an EY roundtable invite).
Close date has slipped three times over the opportunity's 12-month life (31 Dec 2025 to 26 Feb 2027) with no buyer-driven trigger cited for any push.
An EY-partner roundtable re-engagement play was floated for Nov 2025 but no attendance or outcome was ever logged.

The current Next Step claims a Gartner Conference meeting in May 2026 (~2 months ago) where Sasol was 'interested in a follow up,' but no task, call, or email corroborates this and no follow-up has been logged since.

customer_commitment.text: Sasol made two dated commitments -- conclude its internal evaluation of the 20 Aug 2025 demo, and issue the planned RFP -- and has honored neither as of this sweep (9 Jul 2026).
Sasol did accept and populate a Zycus Showpad shared space (5 contacts invited, 22 Aug 2025), the one piece of tangible buyer-side follow-through on record.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The single demo emphasized Zycus' GenAI capabilities and drew good engagement with zero fit gaps identified, but there has been no subsequent AI-specific validation (no RFP was issued to score AI requirements formally).

items.quote: Reduce sourcing plan development from 6 months to 1 month, with basic strategy developed in less than 60 minutes.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The single demo produced a clean fit read (no gaps identified), and Sasol's stated objectives map to Zycus' GenAI sourcing-plan capability, but none of this has been validated in a second session or against a formal requirements document.
